title: erigeron nanus subsp. hungaricus
intro: Erigeron nanus subsp. hungaricus, also known as the Hungarian fleabane or small erigeron, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Erigeron nanus subsp. hungaricus include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Erigeron nanus subsp. hungaricus can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
